annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58538,"title":"[Extensions] Docs: Update README for Azure.Identity → Azure.Core migration in 1.14.0","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` **Service directory:** `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/` **Triggered by:** PR #58520 by `@m-nash` ## What Changed The 1.14.0 release adopted a new `Azure.Core` version that includes identity types (e.g., `ClientSecretCredential`, `EnvironmentCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`) that were previously distributed exclusively in `Azure.Identity`. The `Azure.Identity` package is no longer a direct dependency. ## Gaps Found - The README code snippets use `ClientSecretCredential` and `EnvironmentCredential` without clarifying that these types now come from `Azure.Core` (≥ 1.53) — users may be confused about whether they still need to install `Azure.Identity` separately - The README does not have a **Prerequisites** subsection (required .NET version, whether `Azure.Identity` is still optional, etc.) - The README is missing the **Key concepts** section (explaining `AzureClientFactoryBuilder`, `IAzureClientFactory`, `AzureComponentFactory`, `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`) - The README is missing a **Troubleshooting** section - The README is missing a **Next steps** section 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md` 1. **Add a Prerequisites subsection** inside _Getting started_, after _Install the package_ and before _Register clients_: ```markdown ### Prerequisites - An [Azure subscription]((azure.microsoft.com/redacted) - A compatible .NET runtime (see [Azure SDK for .NET target frameworks]((azure.github.io/redacted) > **Note:** Starting with version 1.14.0, authentication types such as `ClientSecretCredential`, `EnvironmentCredential`, and `ManagedIdentityCredential` are included in `Azure.Core` (≥ 1.53.0) and no longer require a separate `Azure.Identity` package reference. If you have an existing `Azure.Identity` reference in your project, it can be removed unless you use identity types not yet included in `Azure.Core`. ``` 2. **Add a Key concepts section** after the _Getting started_ section: ```markdown ## Key concepts - **`AddAzureClients`** – Extension method on `IServiceCollection` that registers one or more Azure SDK clients with the dependency injection container. - **`IAzureClientFactory`** – Interface that allows resolving named client instances at runtime. - **`AzureComponentFactory`** – Low-level factory for creating `TokenCredential`, `ClientOptions`, and client instances directly from `IConfiguration`. - **`AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`** – Bridges Azure SDK `EventSource`-based logs into the ASP.NET Core `ILogger` pipeline. Call `Start()` on startup and dispose on shutdown. - **Configuration-based credentials** – Clients can be constructed entirely from `IConfiguration` by setting well-known keys such as `credential`, `clientId`, `clientSecret`, `tenantId`, etc. See the [README credential reference](#register-clients) for supported values. ``` 3. **Add a Troubleshooting section** after _Examples_ / _Contributing_: ```markdown ## Troubleshooting - **`InvalidOperationException: Unable to find a constructor`** – Ensure the configuration section passed to `AddXxxClient` contains a key whose name matches a constructor parameter (e.g., `serviceUri` for `BlobServiceClient`). See [AzureComponentFactory]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - **Authentication failures** – Verify that the `credential` key in configuration matches one of the supported values (`default`, `environment`, `managedidentity`, `workloadidentity`, `azurepipelines`, `managedidentityasfederatedidentity`). Starting with 1.14.0, identity types are resolved from `Azure.Core`; remove any conflicting `Azure.Identity` references if you see `CS0433` ambiguous-reference errors. - **Logging** – Enable Azure SDK diagnostic logs in ASP.NET Core by calling `services.AddAzureClientsCore()` (which wires up `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder` automatically) or by manually starting an `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder` instance. ``` 4. **Add a Next steps section**: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure SDK for .NET documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Microsoft.Extensions.Azure API reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Core credential types]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Dependency injection in ASP.NET Core]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet regions are required for the prerequisite note or the key concepts section — they are pure prose. The existing `ConfigureServices`, `Inject`, and `ResolveNamed` snippets remain valid. If you want to add a short snippet demonstrating the `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder`, add a method to a new or existing sample file at: `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/tests/Samples/Sample_LogForwarder.cs` ````csharp #region Snippet:StartLogForwarder // Call once during application startup (e.g., inside Program.cs / Startup.cs) var logForwarder = app.Services.GetRequiredService(); logForwarder.Start(); #endregion ``` Then add a placeholder in the Troubleshooting or Key concepts section: ```` ```` ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current state of `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md`: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Getting started > Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Implicit (covered in Register clients) — acceptable as-is | | Key concepts | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Examples | ⚠️ Covered inline under Getting started; could be a separate section but not blocking | | Troubleshooting | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Next steps | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 extensions` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; inspect git diff to confirm README updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 extensions` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24812803693/agentic_workflow) · ● 327.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58538","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T02:13:16Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:13:16Z"}

could be a separate section but not blocking | | Troubleshooting | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Next steps | ❌ Missing — add per Step 1 | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 extensions` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; {"total_count":33,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3999485256,"number":56583,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[QUERY] Clarify relation to Microsoft.Extensions.Azure","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Core 1.51.0

### Query/Question

Hi,

Could you please clarify how the new [configuration and dependency injection support](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55491) relates to [Microsoft.Extensions.Azure](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure)'s support for configuration and dependency injection through its [ClientFactory](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src/Internal/ClientFactory.cs#L95-L340). Reading through [Azure.Core's documentation](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/System.ClientModel/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) on the subject, Microsoft.Extensions.Azure and Azure.Core seem to solve the same problem in incompatible ways, but I might be missing something here.

BR,
Per

### Environment

Details on CreateClient method in IAzureClientFactory","body":"### Library name and version

Microsoft.Extensions.Azure 1.11.0

### Query/Question

```
services.AddAzureClients(builder =>
{
    builder.UseCredential(new DefaultAzureCredential());

    var defaultServiceBusRetryOptions = new ServiceBusRetryOptions
    {
        Mode = ServiceBusRetryMode.Exponential,
        MaxRetries = 5,
        Delay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2),
        MaxDelay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30)
    };

    // Setting up service bus connections.
    builder
    .AddServiceBusClientWithNamespace(<fullyQualifiedNamespace>)
    .WithName(<clientname>)
    // This is to handle failures in the service bus client creation.
    .ConfigureOptions(options =>
    {
        options.RetryOptions = defaultServiceBusRetryOptions;
    });
});
```
I am using the following code to register the service bus clients.

And in my class constructor
`azureSbClientfactory` is a DI'ed instance of `IAzureClientFactory<ServiceBusClient>`

```
azureSbClientfactory.CreateClient(<same Client name as above>)
```

I am trying to understand if for all the times I create my class(transient class registered in the service) with the same name would it create a new service bus client or create the client for the first time and reuse from the next calls? I've tried searching around in the code but couldn't find anything concrete.

If at all create client creates a new client without caching every time what should I do to create the client once and pass the same every time

### Environment
Production

REQ] Expose `ClientFactory.CreateCredential` for direct consumer use","body":"### Library name

Microsoft.Extensions.Azure

### Please describe the feature.

I'd like to be able to construct a `TokenCredential` instance from `IConfiguration` using the same logic that `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` uses today here:

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src/Internal/ClientFactory.cs#L94-L302

This would enable new scenarios for us, such as being able to create the credentials to access Azure AppConfiguration based on previously configured settings in `IConfiguration`.

We are in a situation currently where we need to support both the `VisualStudioCredential` (for local development) as well as the `ClientSecretCredential` for when running in upper environments (`beta`, `staging` and `production`).

I'm aware that using `DefaultAzureCredentials` accomplishes that when paired with environment variables, and this is the setup we have in some of our applications: we set the credential to that, and then add `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` environment variables when needed. This works well but only when using environment variables is possible.

We hit a situation now where we'd like to configure both the tenant ID and the client id in `appsettings.json`, and then use those values, if present, to build the required credential to communicate with Azure AppConfig.

`Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` has this built-in method that can convert a set of keys from `IConfiguration` into the appropriate `TokenCredential` instance based on the presence of required settings etc. However, this can only be tapped currently when working with Azure Clients and `IServiceCollection`.

I opened a related issue last year:
- https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/43434

But here I am again, one year later, with a harsher requirement: we are in a position now where using environment variables as a fallback is less desirable.

I was told by @jsquire in that issue that the team was already looking into better ways of integrating credentials with `IConfiguration`, but I've not yet seen any movement towards that (unless I'm missing some package, in which case please let me know).

While the team decides on a stronger, more comprehensive way to better integrate `IConfiguration` with other Azure modules, wouldn't it be possible to just expose that `ClientFactory.CreateCredential` method either as-is, or just extract it into a `public` `TokenFactory` class that is then reused inside `ClientFactory` and can also be used by consumers of the NuGet package.

In the old issue, I said I was trying to avoid creating custom extensions on our side to do this and favored just using environment variables instead, but now with a few new projects this has become more cumbersome to support so I will likely change my stance on that and just copy the code from `ClientFactory.CreateCredential` into a reusable class library on our own side in the meantime.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"julealgon","id":461579,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2MTU3OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/461579?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/julealgon","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178447860,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client","name":"Client","color":"ffeb77","description":"This TokenCredential of AddBlobServiceClient(...) is always DefaultAzureCredential","body":"### Library name and version

Microsoft.Extensions.Azure 1.11.0

### Describe the bug

Within the `BlobClientBuilderExtensions` the method

```C#
public static IAzureClientBuilder<BlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions> AddBlobServiceClient<TBuilder>(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)
    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential
{
    return builder.RegisterClientFactory<BlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions>((options, token) => new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, token, options));
}
```
is not passing down the provided `TokenCredential`.

### Expected behavior

The provided `TokenCredential` will be used, maybe like this:

```C#
public static IAzureClientBuilder<BlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions> AddBlobServiceClient<TBuilder>(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)
    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential
{
    return builder.RegisterClientFactory<BlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions>((options, _) => new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, tokenCredential, options));
}
```

### Actual behavior

The `DefaultAzureCredential` is always being used, which leads to unnecessary token requests, that mostly cannot be resolved and even throw exceptions when some configuration is missing.

### Reproduction Steps

Add the service to the `IServiceCollection` like this:

```C#
services.AddAzureClients(
    config =>
    {
        config.AddBlobServiceClient(
            new Uri("<url to the storage account>"),
            new AzureCliCredential());
    });
```

### Environment

ClientFactory with TestServer cannot build a client from a configsection","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus 7.18.4

### Describe the bug

I'm trying to build an [integration test](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/test/integration-tests?view=aspnetcore-9.0) and add settings to a WebHostBuilder.

```
_factory = new WebApplicationFactory<Program>().
        WithWebHostBuilder(config =>
        {
            config.UseSettings("ServiceBusConnection:fullyQualifiedNamespace", uriFromTestContainers);
            // other params
        });
```

ServiceBus client is setup with the `ServiceBusConnection` config section.

```
services.AddAzureClients(builder =>
{
     builder.AddServiceBusClient(configuration.GetSection("ServiceBusConnection");
}
```

When resolving the client, it fails.

The issue is that at runtime the configuration section is provided by CommandLineConfigurationProvider and it seems this provider always return a an empty value for the section instead of just being null.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7221a926-64e5-40dc-94e6-d11c8fa48e35)

So the following code fails because the condition evaluate to true
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src/Internal/ClientFactory.cs#L33-L42

This works well outside a test fixture when the configuration is for example provided with env vars or appsettings.json

Would it be possible to test that the value is not empty or that section.GetChildren() is empty ?

### Expected behavior

The client is created from the configuration section.

### Actual behavior

It fails complaining about string empty not a valid connection string

### Reproduction Steps

Describe above

### Environment

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` README following the 1.14.0 release, which adopted a new `Azure.Core` version containing identity types previously from `Azure.Identity`. The issue requests adding Prerequisites, Key concepts, Troubleshooting, and Next steps sections to the README.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` 1.14.0  
**Affected API:** README documentation  
**Scenarios:**
- Users may be confused about whether `Azure.Identity` is still needed after identity types moved to `Azure.Core` ≥ 1.53
- README lacks Prerequisites, Key concepts, Troubleshooting, and Next steps sections per SDK documentation standards

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md` to document the `Azure.Identity` → `Azure.Core` migration impact and add missing standard README sections.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue with no runtime bug. The implementation guide in the issue body provides detailed steps for editing the README and validating with `Update-Snippets.ps1` and `Export-API.ps1`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md` against the SDK documentation standards
2. Confirm which identity types were migrated to `Azure.Core` 1.53 vs. those still in `Azure.Identity`
3. Implement the Prerequisites, Key concepts, Troubleshooting, and Next steps sections per the implementation guide
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 extensions` to inject any snippet placeholders
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` is a client-side integration/extensions package, not an ARM management or Provisioning package
- **Service:** `Extensions` — confirmed by reference issues #49501 and #48368 which both use `Client` + `Extensions` for `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` issues
- **Confidence:** High — the package is explicitly named in the issue body; category and service are unambiguous; prediction aligns with multiple quality reference issues
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Extensions` (line 59) — the only entry matching `%Extensions` exactly
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @jsquire
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@jsquire` (single AzureSdkOwner)
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan encountered line 586 (`# ServiceLabel: %Compute - Extensions`) first — no match since predicted label is "Extensions" not "Compute - Extensions"; then matched line 59 (`# ServiceLabel: %Extensions`)
</details>
